user_query:
I'm visiting Shenyang for the first time and want a small, reliable incident monitor I can run locally before I head out. Please turn the provided skeleton into a working script that discovers and downloads current official advisories relevant to Shenyang without hardcoding direct URLs, then extracts useful incident items and writes structured outputs. Use these inputs: input/monitor.yaml and input/attractions.csv.

Requirements:
1) Modify input/monitor.yaml if needed to ensure detection_keywords cover both English and Chinese terms and that the reliability settings (timeouts, retries, backoff) are applied by the code.
2) Implement and update scripts/fetcher.py so it does all of the following:
   - Discover and download webpage content for three official sources identified by objective attributes only (no direct hardcoded URL):
     a) Shenyang Metro service advisories (official metro operator notices page; Chinese-language; organization name includes Shenyang Metro Group; site title contains "Shenyang Metro" or "沈阳地铁"; typical sections include 公告/服务公告/运营信息)
     b) Shenyang Municipal Government notices (official municipal portal; Chinese-language; organization includes Shenyang Municipal Government; site title contains "Shenyang" or "沈阳"; typical sections include 通知公告/应急/新闻)
     c) China Railway/12306 service updates relevant to Shenyang (official railway service updates; Chinese-language; organization includes China Railway/12306; site title contains "12306" or "铁路"; typical sections include 公告/服务/出行提示)
   - Use resilient fetching: apply the timeout, retries, and backoff from input/monitor.yaml.
   - Save raw HTML snapshots for each attempted source under data/raw with filenames of the form "{source_name}_{YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ}.html".
   - Extract advisory-like items from each source page: titles or headings, any visible links, and any text matching detection_keywords. Do not rely on exact page wording; fall back to conservative extraction if needed.
   - Cross-check each extracted item against input/attractions.csv by keyword matching (attraction names and nearby_keywords). Flag impacted attractions if any keyword overlaps.
   - Write structured results to data/processed/incidents.json containing an object with these fields: run_id, trip_date, retrieved_at_utc, sources (array of per-source objects). Each per-source object must include: source_name, fetch_status (ok or failed), raw_snapshot_path, advisory_count, items (array). Each item must include: title, detected_keywords (array), links (array of absolute URLs if available), impact_on_attractions (array of attraction names).
   - Also write an aggregate summary CSV to data/processed/incidents_summary.csv with columns: source_name, fetch_status, advisory_count.
   - Append a plain-text log file at logs/fetch.log with at least one line per source stating start/end and whether the fetch succeeded.
3) Ensure the outputs are created in the specified paths and are non-empty even if no advisories are found (write advisory_count=0 and include fetch_status for each source).

Inputs: input/monitor.yaml, input/attractions.csv. Outputs to create: data/raw/*.html snapshots, data/processed/incidents.json, data/processed/incidents_summary.csv, logs/fetch.log.

Please keep the internet discovery grounded in the official sources described above by their names and attributes only, and make sure your code respects and uses the configuration values from input/monitor.yaml. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
